title: Bind releases to the remote default branch HEAD
outOfScope: Product source code and unrelated release features

sources.reason: Defines Work Item 5 release source binding objective and required checks.
Aligns release evidence, remote default branch, and fail-closed terminology.
Current release workflow source selection and evidence boundary.
Current release distribution and evidence validation implementation.
Shared Git and remote metadata helpers used by release validation.
Workflow contract tests for release source behavior.
Release evidence and distribution validation tests.
Documents release provenance and distribution expectations.

riskAssessment.reason: Release provenance is high impact; source resolution must fail closed when remote default branch metadata is unavailable.

executionDecision.reason: The user authorized the implementation loop, including release workflow, governance scripts, tests, and documentation within this Contract scope.

checkpointPolicy.reason: Record at least one checkpoint before finishing to reduce mid-task drift.

acceptance: A1: Release workflow resolves the remote default branch and rejects a feature-branch or ambiguous source.
A2: Release validation fails closed when Remote HEAD/default branch metadata is missing and accepts an explicit source only when it equals origin/<default-branch>.
A3: Release Evidence records Source Commit, Remote, Default Branch, and Run ID with validation coverage.
A4: Workflow/release tests and distribution documentation cover success, feature-branch failure, missing HEAD failure, and explicit-base behavior.
guidelines: Release source selection must be repository-relative and fail closed; never infer provenance from a moving local branch.
Keep remote/default-branch resolution in shared Git helpers and preserve existing release evidence compatibility.
Do not publish or mutate external release state as part of this Work Item; implementation and verification remain local/CI scoped.

intent.problem: Release workflows can currently validate or package from an unintended local or feature branch, weakening provenance and making published artifacts difficult to audit.
constraints: The authoritative source is the remote default branch HEAD, not the current checkout branch.
Missing or ambiguous remote metadata must fail closed.
Preserve explicit base/source validation and historic release evidence compatibility.
intent.rationale: Binding release source to remote default-branch HEAD makes provenance deterministic and prevents a feature branch from being mistaken for publishable release input.

scenarioCoverage.reason: Release source is rejected when it differs from the remote default branch HEAD.

scenarioCoverage.scenario: Remote default branch HEAD is accepted
scenarioCoverage.reason: The authoritative release source remains usable when identities match.

scenarioCoverage.scenario: Missing remote HEAD/default branch metadata fails closed
scenarioCoverage.reason: Missing remote identity fields are rejected.

scenarioCoverage.scenario: Explicit base is accepted only when bound to the default branch
scenarioCoverage.reason: Explicit source evidence is accepted only when all binding fields are present and matched.

restrictedWriteApproval.reason: The user explicitly authorized the complete Work Item loop, including release workflow, governance scripts, tests, documentation, branch operations, PR merge, and lifecycle cleanup.

rollbackNote: Revert this Work Item diff and restore related tests and docs.
